title: Message Envelopes, Binary Bodies, Schemas, and Evolution · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for message envelopes, binary bodies, schemas, and evolution and requires the smallest defensible versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.
Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them.
Validate unknown envelopes and payloads with strict runtime schemas, bounded collections, semantic refinements, and explicit rejection evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Buffer Decoding Boundary · changed case · Quiz Buffer Decoding Boundary
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 6359 in a accessible municipal task intake with two worker pools. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz messaging team handles case 7920 in a accessible municipal task intake with two worker pools. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AsyncAPI 3.1 and CloudEvents 1.0.2 selectively to document channels, operations, messages, bindings, examples, and event metadata while testing runtime truth.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A valid AsyncAPI document proves deployed routing and message behavior conform.
